items.title: Who is John Ternus, the incoming Apple CEO?
items.description: Starting on September 1, Ternus will lead one of the world's most valuable companies, but if you're not a dedicated Apple enthusiast, you've probably never heard of this man, who has largely remained out of the spotlight until now.

items.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
items.description: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.

items.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in 7 new countries
items.description: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam. The company is rolling this feature out to both desktop and iOS in all of these countries except Japan.

items.title: Tim Cook stepping down as Apple CEO, John Ternus taking over
items.description: The transition has been expected for some time and ends one of the longer and more impactful runs a CEO has had at any company. Cook had inherited a company that many industry watchers and enthusiasts struggled to separate from its famed founder. Wha

items.title: Google Photos adds new touch-up tools for ‘quick’ fixes
items.description: You can use the new tools to remove blemishes, refine skin texture, brighten eyes, or whiten teeth in photos.

items.title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing
items.description: This sentence construction ("It's not just this — it's that") has become so common in AI-generated writing that it's no longer just a clue that a piece of writing may be synthetic — it's almost a guarantee.

items.title: North Korean hackers blamed for $290M crypto theft
items.description: The hack against Kelp DAO is the largest crypto heist of the year so far.

items.title: Mastodon says its flagship server was hit by a DDoS attack
items.description: The DDoS attack against Mastodon's flagship server comes less than a week after Bluesky was targeted with junk web traffic.

items.title: FAA orders investigation into Blue Origin’s New Glenn mishap
items.description: The New Glenn rocket will have to stay grounded while the company investigates what caused the apparent failure of its upper stage.

items.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
items.description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

items.title: WhatsApp is testing a premium subscription, but it is mainly cosmetic
items.description: WhatsApp is testing a subscription after Meta debuted a similar subscription for Instagram earlier this year.

items.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
items.description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

items.title: Deezer says 44% of songs uploaded to its platform daily are AI-generated
items.description: Deezer says consumption of AI-generated music on the platform is still very low, between 1% and 3% of the total streams, and 85% of these streams are detected as fraudulent and are demonetized.

items.title: App host Vercel says it was hacked and customer data stolen
items.description: Vercel blamed its breach on an earlier hack at Context AI, which allowed hackers to hijack a Vercel employee's account to steal customer data.

items.title: Rivian’s factory hit by tornado ahead of R2 launch
items.description: The tornado hit a newer part of the factory, primarily used for R2 logistics such as deliveries of parts. Rivian has paused operations in the building and expects to start back up sometime this week.

items.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
items.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

items.title: The 12-month window
items.description: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

items.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn put a customer satellite in the wrong orbit during its third launch
items.description: The first major failure of Blue Origin's new heavy-launch system could create delays to its ambition to help NASA and the Trump administration return to the moon.

items.title: Robots beat human records at Beijing half-marathon
items.description: The winning time is a massive improvement over last year’s race, when the fastest robot finished in two hours and 40 minutes.

items.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
items.description: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."
